questions.prompt: The fundamental definition of Q is:
options: Q = 2π × (stored energy / energy dissipated per cycle)
questions.correctAnswer: Q = 2π × (stored energy / energy dissipated per cycle)
questions.explanation: This is the physics-level definition: Q = 2π × wS/wD. All other Q formulas (ωoL/R, ωo/B, etc.) derive from this.

questions.prompt: At resonance, the total stored energy in L and C is:
questions.explanation: wL + wC = Vm²L/(2R²) = constant. Energy oscillates between L and C but the sum is always the same.

questions.prompt: When the inductor energy is maximum, the capacitor energy is:
questions.explanation: wL ∝ cos²(ωt), wC ∝ sin²(ωt). When wL is max (cos²=1), wC is zero (sin²=0). Energy has fully transferred to the magnetic field.

questions.prompt: Which of these is NOT an equivalent expression for Q?
questions.explanation: For series: Q = ωoL/R (not R/ωoL). R/ωoL is the PARALLEL Q formula. Don't mix them up!

questions.prompt: If Q=200 and Vs=10V, the voltage across the capacitor at resonance is:
questions.explanation: |VC| = Q × Vs = 200 × 10 = 2000V. This is voltage magnification — the capacitor must be rated above this value.

questions.prompt: A crystal oscillator typically has Q of:
questions.explanation: Quartz crystals have Q > 10,000 (sometimes > 100,000). This extreme selectivity enables precise frequency generation for clocks.
Q = ω₀/B. Higher Q → smaller B → narrower passband → better frequency discrimination.

questions.prompt: The energy exchange at resonance is analogous to:
questions.explanation: Inductor (magnetic energy) ↔ capacitor (electric energy), like kinetic energy ↔ potential energy in a pendulum. Total energy constant.

questions.prompt: Real inductors reduce Q from the ideal value because:
questions.explanation: Real inductors have parasitic resistance from the wire winding. This adds to R, reducing Q = ωoL/R_total.

questions.prompt: To achieve high Q in a series RLC circuit, you need:
questions.explanation: Q = ωoL/R. To maximise Q: minimise R (less loss) and maximise L (more energy storage per cycle).
